title: Personal identity privacy and data ownership family defined
inputs: LIFEOS-008 (personal capability list: identity, privacy, memory, planning, health, finance, legal, files, calendar, contacts, communications)
INTEL-001 (governed data-substrate classes: hot/warm/cold memory, relational, vector, graph, embedded-ledger, artifact, identity, provenance)
Existing personal app surfaces (data.js: personal workspace sections + Settings/Profile panels)
Architecture Blueprint - LifeOS Core Foundation.md (foundational source of truth)
objective: Assign every personal LifeOS data class exactly one user-controlled ownership, consent gate, storage tier, encryption boundary, access rule, retention tier, export/sharing path, provenance mechanism, deletion authority, backup, recovery source, and proof task, on the STORE-001 tiered authority model (PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical durable, redb transient, AgentDB per-agent cognition, OS keyring for secrets, envctl forward-only bridge/projection), such that no personal data crosses a boundary silently.
grounding_note: Local-first == the canonical durable owner (PostgreSQL+RuVector) runs on the user's own edge node under the 'No Sidecar' / install-in-place musl model in the Blueprint; personal data therefore never requires a network hop off-device to be authoritative. Every storage assignment below is the personal-domain instantiation of a STORE-001 state class; no new store or authority is invented and no class is assigned two durable owners.

invariants_inherited: Single durable writer: PostgreSQL owns each durable personal class exactly once; redb is never a second durable writer.
Forward-only sync: redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write.
Verified cutover: a class leaves redb-durable only after a green blobstore_parity.rs (SHA-256 identity) run; never by assumption.
No sidecar: RuVector runs as a native PostgreSQL extension; no separate vector DB holds personal semantics.
Provenance preserved: SHA-256/SHAKE256 identity + source lineage on every durable class.
store_001_alignment.mapping_note: A class may touch several tiers (e.g. health raw samples in redb, settled records in PostgreSQL, embeddings in RuVector) but has exactly ONE canonical durable owner named in its work_item; multi-tier presence is the intended STORE-001 pipeline, not divided authority.

personal_data_classes_enumerated: PERSONAL-DC-11 Personal agent cognition & chat memory

work_items.surface: data.js profile/Profile (Account alex@lifeos.ai, avatar & display name, language & region) + hubs Identity model (values / habits / preferences, line 328)

owner.user_authority: user is sovereign owner of their identity record
owner.write_authority: PostgreSQL single durable writer; user-initiated edits only

work_items.decision: Canonical identity (legal/display name, contact handle, avatar ref, locale) is a durable relational macro-state row owned once by PostgreSQL; the learned values/habits/preferences model is per-agent cognition in AgentDB (.rvf), non-authoritative and witnessed back to the PostgreSQL identity source vector.
work_items.consent: Identity record created only by explicit user action; no field is inferred and persisted without an on-record consent grant logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.

storage.transient_role: AgentDB .rvf holds the derived preference model; never a durable authority for the identity of record

work_items.encryption: At rest on the user's local edge PostgreSQL under keys held in the OS keyring (STORE-001 external-secret authority); no raw identity secret stored in PostgreSQL columns.
work_items.access: User + explicitly authorized personal agents; MinCut isolates any agent that drifts before it can read identity rows.
work_items.retention: Retained for account lifetime; superseded fields versioned, not silently overwritten; agent preference model expires with the agent .rvf.
work_items.provenance: module_path/row lineage in PostgreSQL; agent preference claims bound to SHAKE256 witness chains rooted in the identity source vector (Causal Graph Verification rejects unrooted preference edits).
work_items.export_sharing: Export only via envctl projection to a user-requested file; sharing a profile field requires an explicit consent grant recorded in PERSONAL-DC-12.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; PostgreSQL DELETE plus AgentDB .rvf discard; deletion event tombstoned in PERSONAL-DC-12 (provenance of deletion preserved, data not).
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR (local edge); agent model regenerable from base model + training history.
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR restore of the identity row; agent preference model re-derived.
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: identity leaves the device only through a witnessed, consented envctl edge; agent model stays in the local .rvf.
work_items.sync_edge: user edit -> PostgreSQL (durable); PostgreSQL identity vector -> AgentDB derives preference model (forward-only, non-authoritative)

work_items.surface: data.js profile Logins & passwords (password vault, passkeys, two-factor, active sessions) + Secrets & keys (API keys, SSH keys, PGP keys, SSL certificates)

owner.user_authority: user is sole owner and rotation authority for every secret

work_items.decision: Raw secrets live ONLY in the OS keyring; PostgreSQL holds non-secret references/provenance (service name, key id, last-rotated, session device) and NEVER the secret material; redb NEVER holds secrets.
work_items.consent: Secret stored on explicit user entry/import; use of a secret by any agent requires a per-use consent scope logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.

storage.transient_role: none; redb and PostgreSQL are barred from holding raw secrets
storage.lifetime: until rotated or revoked by the user

work_items.encryption: OS keyring native encryption (service 'lifeos'); PostgreSQL stores only references, so no plaintext secret ever reaches the durable relational tier.
work_items.access: User + narrowly scoped agent grants; active-session list is user-revocable; MinCut severs any agent requesting out-of-scope secrets.
work_items.retention: Retained until user rotation/revocation; revoked secrets removed from keyring and tombstoned (reference only) in PERSONAL-DC-12.
work_items.provenance: Committed source pins + keyring account references; STORE-001 external-secret class migration_path = none.
work_items.export_sharing: Secrets are non-exportable by default; any share is an explicit, witnessed, user-authorized keyring action, never a bulk export.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; keyring delete + reference tombstone; no PITR retention of secret material (secrets never entered PITR scope).
work_items.backup: User-controlled keyring backup only; LifeOS does not replicate secrets into PostgreSQL PITR.
work_items.egress_control: Hard boundary: secrets never leave the keyring into PostgreSQL, redb, projections, embeddings, or the network; this is the strongest no-silent-egress class.
work_items.sync_edge: n/a (isolated authority); only non-secret provenance references flow keyring -> PostgreSQL

work_items.surface: data.js personal Health (Sleep / Activity / Heart, view: health) + HealthView + personal Files pf-health (lab-results.pdf) + aggregator health score 92

owner.user_authority: user owns all biometric and clinical data
owner.store_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (settled records + artifacts + trend embeddings)
owner.write_authority: PostgreSQL single durable writer; redb buffers raw samples pre-flush

work_items.decision: High-frequency biometric samples land in redb as transient buffer; envctl flushes settled/aggregated health states forward to PostgreSQL (canonical durable); lab-result artifacts are content-addressed blobs (SHA-256) in PostgreSQL BlobStore; health-trend semantics are RuVector embeddings in PostgreSQL.
work_items.consent: Sensor ingestion and clinical-record storage each require an explicit health-data consent grant; withdrawal stops ingestion and is logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.

storage.tier: transient high-frequency (redb raw samples) -> durable relational + artifact + semantic (PostgreSQL+RuVector)
storage.transient_role: redb high-frequency sample buffer + BLAKE3 dedup cache; process-lifecycle only
storage.lifetime: raw samples transient (redb); settled records durable (PostgreSQL PITR)

work_items.encryption: PostgreSQL at rest on local edge under keyring-held keys; redb sample buffer is a local memory-mapped file never networked; lab-result blobs encrypted at rest.
work_items.access: User + explicitly authorized care/coach agents; MinCut isolation guards clinical rows.
work_items.retention: Raw samples retained only until aggregated then dropped from redb; settled clinical records retained per user-set retention tier with PITR; artifacts retained until user deletes.
work_items.provenance: SHA-256 identity on lab-result blobs (BlobStore parity contract); row lineage + source device provenance on samples.
work_items.export_sharing: Export to a clinician is an explicit envctl projection of selected records/artifacts under a consent grant; no automatic upstream sharing.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; PostgreSQL DELETE of records + blob removal; redb transient loss is by-definition; deletion tombstoned in PERSONAL-DC-12.
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR (records + artifacts); redb samples not backed up (transient by design).
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR + reproducible re-embedding of trends; raw samples not recoverable (transient).
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: biometric data reaches PostgreSQL only via the forward-only envctl flush; leaves the device only via a witnessed, consented projection.
work_items.sync_edge: sensors -> redb buffer (BLAKE3 dedup) -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL+RuVector (forward-only)

work_items.surface: data.js personal Finance (Net worth, Cash flow) + Wallet (Chase Checking $12,450.32, Cards) + personal Files pf-finance (budget-2026.json, receipts.json)

owner.user_authority: user owns all financial records and balances
owner.write_authority: PostgreSQL single durable writer (ACID is required for money)

work_items.decision: The financial ledger (balances, transactions, net-worth history) is a durable ACID relational class owned once by PostgreSQL (INTEL-001 embedded-ledger class); budget/receipt artifacts are SHA-256 content-addressed blobs; rate-limited balance polls buffer transiently in redb and flush forward.
work_items.consent: Account linking and balance polling require explicit user consent; each linked institution credential is a PERSONAL-DC-02 keyring secret, not a ledger column.

storage.tier: durable relational (ACID ledger) + artifact; transient poll buffer in redb
storage.transient_role: redb buffers high-frequency balance polls / provider responses before forward flush

work_items.encryption: PostgreSQL at rest under keyring-held keys; institution credentials isolated in keyring (PERSONAL-DC-02); no bank credential in the ledger tier.
work_items.access: User + explicitly authorized finance agents (Finance Bench); MinCut guards ledger rows against drifting agents.
work_items.retention: Ledger retained per user retention tier with full PITR history (financial audit needs durability); transient polls dropped after flush.
work_items.provenance: SHA-256 on artifacts; append-with-history ledger lineage; every automated transaction categorization is a witnessed edit (SHAKE256).
work_items.export_sharing: Export (e.g. tax package) is an explicit envctl projection under consent; no automatic third-party transmission.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; PostgreSQL DELETE / archival with tombstone in PERSONAL-DC-12; ACID guarantees no partial-state loss.
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR (local edge); artifacts in BlobStore.
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR restore of the ledger to any point in time.
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: balances reach PostgreSQL only via forward-only envctl flush; institution tokens never leave the keyring; export is witnessed.
work_items.sync_edge: provider poll -> redb buffer -> envctl -> PostgreSQL ledger (forward-only)

work_items.surface: data.js personal Legal (Will & estate, Insurance auto-renews Aug)
work_items.decision: Legal document metadata (type, status, renewal date, parties) is durable relational state in PostgreSQL; the documents themselves are tamper-evident SHA-256 content-addressed blobs (BlobStore parity), witnessed by SHAKE256 chains so a legal document cannot be silently altered.
work_items.consent: Documents stored on explicit user upload; sharing with an executor/attorney requires an explicit, scoped consent grant logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.
work_items.encryption: PostgreSQL + blob at rest under keyring-held keys; local-first, never off-device without a witnessed export.
work_items.access: User + explicitly named agents/parties; MinCut isolation; access events witnessed.
work_items.retention: Long-horizon retention set by the user; superseded versions kept as content-addressed history, not overwritten.
work_items.provenance: SHA-256 document identity + SHAKE256 witness chain (tamper evidence) + source pin; Causal Graph Verification rejects unrooted edits.
work_items.export_sharing: Export to an authorized party is an explicit witnessed envctl projection under a scoped, revocable consent grant.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; PostgreSQL DELETE + blob removal + PERSONAL-DC-12 tombstone; version history retained until user purges.
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR + re-materialization of the exact bytes by SHA-256 (exact-byte materialization capability).
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: legal documents leave only via a witnessed, consented projection; tamper attempts fail witness-chain verification.
work_items.sync_edge: user upload -> PostgreSQL + BlobStore (durable, witnessed); no reverse authority

work_items.surface: data.js personal Files (Browse files, view: files, 5 folders / 12 recent) + files.personal (pf-finance, pf-health folders; resume.pdf, receipts.json)

owner.user_authority: user owns the personal file vault
owner.store_owner: PostgreSQL BlobStore (canonical content) with envctl filesystem projection (derived view)
owner.write_authority: PostgreSQL single durable writer; envctl projects read-only to filesystem

work_items.decision: File content is canonical as SHA-256 content-addressed blobs in PostgreSQL BlobStore (ADOPT-001 exact-byte materialization); the browsable/downloadable filesystem view is a DERIVED, disposable envctl projection (STORE-001 derived-projections class), regenerated deterministically from module_path metadata.
work_items.consent: Files stored on explicit user action; the projection is local and consent-gated; no file is projected to a shared location without a grant logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.

storage.transient_role: envctl filesystem projection is derived and disposable, never a second authority

work_items.encryption: Blobs at rest under keyring-held keys; projections written only into the FS scope $APPDATA/lifeos/* (locked Tauri scope), never outside.
work_items.access: User + explicitly authorized agents; projection confined to the locked FS scope; MinCut isolation.
work_items.retention: Content retained per user retention tier; projections are ephemeral and re-projected on demand.
work_items.provenance: SHA-256 content identity + module_path lineage; every file traceable to its source commit/upload.
work_items.export_sharing: Export/download is an explicit envctl projection under consent; no background sync out of the FS scope.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; PostgreSQL blob delete + projection cleanup + PERSONAL-DC-12 tombstone.
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR / BlobStore; projections not backed up (re-projectable).
work_items.recovery: Re-projection from PostgreSQL by SHA-256 (exact-byte materialization); PITR for the content store.
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: the only filesystem exit is the envctl projection into the locked $APPDATA/lifeos scope; anything leaving that scope requires a witnessed, consented action.
work_items.sync_edge: user upload -> PostgreSQL BlobStore (durable); PostgreSQL -> envctl materialize -> filesystem (forward-only, derived)

work_items.surface: data.js personal Calendar (Soccer pickup, Reading hour, view: calendar) + calendar aggregator + planning tasks

owner.user_authority: user owns their calendar and plans
owner.write_authority: PostgreSQL single durable writer; redb buffers reminder fire state

work_items.decision: Events, tasks and plans are durable relational rows owned once by PostgreSQL; high-frequency reminder/notification fire state (imminent alerts, dismiss flags) is transient in redb and never a durable authority.
work_items.consent: Calendar sources connected on explicit user consent; cross-workspace aggregation is a local read, not an external share.

storage.tier: durable relational (events/tasks); transient high-frequency (reminder state in redb)

work_items.encryption: PostgreSQL at rest under keyring-held keys; redb reminder buffer local-only.
work_items.access: User + authorized planning agents; shared-event visibility is per-event and consent-scoped; MinCut isolation.
work_items.retention: Events retained per user retention tier; past events archivable; reminder flags dropped after fire.
work_items.provenance: Row lineage + source-calendar provenance; imported events keep their origin reference.
work_items.export_sharing: Publishing/sharing an event requires an explicit consent grant logged to PERSONAL-DC-12; no default outbound publish.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; PostgreSQL DELETE + PERSONAL-DC-12 tombstone; redb reminder state transient.
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR; reminder state not backed up (transient).
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: calendar data stays in PostgreSQL; any external publish is witnessed and consented.
work_items.sync_edge: user/import -> PostgreSQL (durable); reminder fire -> redb (transient); no reverse authority

work_items.surface: data.js personal Contacts (view: contacts, 18 people) + contacts.personal (GP Dr. Amara Diallo, school contact) + hubs contacts + contacts aggregator

owner.user_authority: user owns their contacts and relationship graph
owner.store_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (relational records + GNN relationship edges + semantic search)

work_items.decision: Contact records are durable relational rows in PostgreSQL; the relationship/knowledge graph (who-relates-to-whom, causal edges) and semantic contact search are RuVector GNN + vector state native in PostgreSQL (No Sidecar); no separate contact/vector store.
work_items.consent: Contacts imported on explicit user consent; enrichment (linking a contact across channels) is a witnessed local operation, not an external lookup, unless the user grants it.

storage.transient_role: redb may cache f32 distance math for semantic search only (no authority)

work_items.encryption: PostgreSQL at rest under keyring-held keys; contact channel credentials (if any) isolated in keyring (PERSONAL-DC-02).
work_items.access: User + authorized agents; MinCut isolation; relationship-graph reads are access-logged for sensitive contacts.
work_items.retention: Retained per user retention tier; deletable per-contact.
work_items.provenance: Row lineage + source-import provenance; graph edges are witnessed (Causal Graph Verification) so no fabricated relationship persists.
work_items.export_sharing: Sharing a contact/vCard is an explicit consented, witnessed export; no bulk contact upload by default.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; per-contact PostgreSQL DELETE + graph-edge removal + PERSONAL-DC-12 tombstone.
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: contacts and relationship edges stay in PostgreSQL; any export is witnessed and consented; RuVector stays in-DB (no sidecar exfiltration surface).

work_items.surface: data.js personal Family (Partner 'At home · Nearby', Kids 'At school · Safe zone', Parents 'Boston · 3:30 PM', Family hub shared calendar + tasks)

owner.user_authority: user + co-owning family members share authority; presence is multi-party data
owner.store_owner: redb (transient presence buffer) -> PostgreSQL (settled shared-hub records)
owner.write_authority: redb per-device for raw presence; PostgreSQL single durable writer for settled shared records

work_items.decision: Raw presence/location updates (partner nearby, kid at school, parent locale) are transient high-frequency state in redb (STORE-001 transient class) - location is transient by nature; only SETTLED, significant states (safe-zone entry/exit events, shared-hub calendar/tasks) are flushed forward to PostgreSQL as durable multi-party records under multi-party consent.
work_items.consent: Presence sharing requires explicit multi-party consent (each tracked member, guardians for minors); revocation by any party stops that member's sharing and is logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.

storage.tier: transient high-frequency (redb presence) -> durable relational (PostgreSQL shared-hub records)
storage.canonical_owner: redb for raw presence stream (terminal transient role); PostgreSQL for settled shared records
storage.transient_role: redb is the intended terminal owner of the raw location stream (never durable macro-state)
storage.lifetime: raw presence transient (redb, process-lifecycle); settled events durable (PostgreSQL)

work_items.encryption: redb presence buffer is a local memory-mapped file never networked off the edge node; settled records at rest in PostgreSQL under keyring-held keys.
work_items.access: Only consented family members + explicitly authorized agents; minors' location gated behind guardian consent; MinCut isolates any agent probing presence out of scope.
work_items.retention: Raw presence NOT retained (transient by definition - drops with the process); only settled safe-zone/hub events persist per retention tier.
work_items.provenance: Settled events carry device/source provenance; shared-hub edits witnessed.
work_items.export_sharing: Presence never auto-shares beyond consented family; external sharing is barred without an explicit witnessed grant.
work_items.deletion_authority: Each member is deletion authority for their own presence; PostgreSQL DELETE of settled records + tombstone; raw stream is transient.
work_items.backup: Only settled shared records in PostgreSQL PITR; raw presence not backed up.
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR for settled records; raw presence not recoverable (transient by design).
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: raw location stays in local redb and is never flushed wholesale; only consented, settled, significant events cross to PostgreSQL; minors' data has the strictest boundary.
work_items.sync_edge: device presence -> redb (transient) -> envctl flush of settled events -> PostgreSQL (forward-only, multi-party consented)

work_items.surface: data.js personal Social Media (4 accounts linked, Mentions) + media Socials + communications threads/messages

owner.user_authority: user owns their messages, threads and social records
owner.store_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (durable message/thread records + semantic search); OS keyring (account link tokens)
owner.write_authority: PostgreSQL single durable writer for records; keyring for link tokens

work_items.decision: Message/thread records and social posts are durable relational + semantic state in PostgreSQL+RuVector; social-account OAuth/link tokens are keyring secrets (PERSONAL-DC-02), never message columns; high-frequency mention/notification streams buffer transiently in redb and flush forward.
work_items.consent: Account linking and message ingestion require explicit user consent per account; each linked account credential is a keyring secret; revocation logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.

storage.tier: durable relational + semantic (records) + external-secret (link tokens) + transient (mention stream)
storage.transient_role: redb buffers high-frequency mention/notification stream (no authority)
storage.lifetime: durable records (PITR); tokens until revoked; mention stream transient

work_items.encryption: PostgreSQL at rest under keyring-held keys; link tokens isolated in keyring; redb mention buffer local-only.
work_items.access: User + authorized comms agents; MinCut isolation; per-thread access scoping.
work_items.retention: Messages retained per user retention tier; tokens until revoked; mention stream dropped after flush.
work_items.provenance: Row lineage + source-account provenance; automated replies/classifications witnessed (SHAKE256).
work_items.export_sharing: Export of a thread is an explicit consented, witnessed projection; no re-posting or forwarding without a grant.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; PostgreSQL DELETE of records + keyring token revoke + PERSONAL-DC-12 tombstone.
work_items.backup: PostgreSQL PITR (records); tokens user-managed in keyring.
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR + reproducible re-embedding; tokens re-authorized by the user.
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: outbound posts/messages require an explicit, witnessed, consented send; tokens never leave the keyring; ingestion is forward-only.
work_items.sync_edge: provider -> redb mention buffer -> envctl -> PostgreSQL records; tokens keyring-isolated (forward-only)

work_items.surface: src App.vue AIAvatar + stores/lifeos sendAiMessage / aiMessages + hubs Identity model (agent-learned) + AgentDB .rvf

owner.user_authority: user owns the agent and may reset/delete its cognition
owner.write_authority: AgentDB per agent (self-contained); no durable macro-state authority

work_items.decision: The personal agent's learned cognition (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy) is per-agent AgentDB (.rvf) - non-authoritative, witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors; live chat memory (aiMessages) is EPHEMERAL and is explicitly EXCLUDED from persistence (CLAUDE.md whitelist: aiMessages excluded to avoid replaying stale chat).
work_items.consent: Agent learning-on-user-data requires an explicit consent grant; chat is ephemeral by default; persisting any conversational memory is opt-in and logged to PERSONAL-DC-12.

storage.transient_role: redb may scratchpad agent thoughts (transient); chat buffer is in-memory only

work_items.encryption: AgentDB .rvf is a single local file; witness chains bind claims to PostgreSQL source vectors; no chat persisted to disk.
work_items.access: The owning agent + user; MinCut severs a hallucinating agent's cognition from the shared graph.
work_items.retention: Cognition retained in the .rvf until reset; chat NOT retained (excluded from LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS).
work_items.provenance: SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chains root every learned claim in a real PostgreSQL source vector; Causal Graph Verification rejects hallucinated (unrooted) memory.
work_items.export_sharing: Agent cognition is local to the .rvf; sharing/exporting an agent is an explicit user action; chat cannot leak because it is never persisted.
work_items.deletion_authority: User; discard/reset the .rvf; chat clears on session end; deletion of a persisted-memory opt-in tombstoned in PERSONAL-DC-12.
work_items.backup: AgentDB .rvf regenerable from base model + training history; chat not backed up (ephemeral).
work_items.recovery: Re-derive the .rvf from the base model + PostgreSQL-rooted history; chat not recoverable (by design).
work_items.egress_control: No silent egress: cognition stays in the local .rvf; unwitnessed (hallucinated) claims are rejected at write; ephemeral chat cannot leave because it is never stored or synced.
work_items.sync_edge: PostgreSQL source vectors -> AgentDB derives cognition (forward-only, witnessed, non-authoritative); chat: in-memory only, no sync

work_items.surface: Cross-cutting: consent grants/revocations, access events, egress/share events, deletion tombstones for all PERSONAL-DC-01..11 (INTEL-001 provenance class; embedded-ledger)

owner.user_authority: user owns and can read the full audit of their own data
owner.store_owner: PostgreSQL (durable append-only ledger) with OS keyring source pins
owner.write_authority: PostgreSQL single durable writer; append-only, witnessed

work_items.decision: A durable append-only provenance/consent/egress ledger in PostgreSQL records every consent grant/revocation, access, cross-boundary movement, and deletion tombstone for all other classes; each entry is bound to a SHAKE256 witness chain and Causal Graph edge. This ledger is the enforcement substrate for 'no data leaves its boundary silently'.
work_items.consent: The ledger itself is user-readable; recording an event is mandatory (not optional) for any consent/access/egress/deletion on any other class.

storage.transient_role: none (the ledger is the durable source of audit truth)
storage.lifetime: durable, long-horizon (PITR); append-only, never silently mutated

work_items.encryption: PostgreSQL at rest under keyring-held keys; witness roots + source pins committed.
work_items.access: User (full read of own audit) + governance/privacy agent; entries tamper-evident.
work_items.retention: Retained long-horizon per user retention tier; append-only so history is complete; deletion tombstones outlive the deleted data (provenance-of-deletion preserved).
work_items.provenance: Self-witnessing: every entry SHAKE256-chained + Causal-Graph-verified; no entry without a real root.
work_items.export_sharing: User may export their own audit trail via a witnessed envctl projection; the ledger is never shared externally by default.
work_items.deletion_authority: Append-only: entries are not deleted; a correction is a new witnessed entry (integrity of the audit trail is preserved).
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR restore of the full ledger.
work_items.egress_control: This class IS the no-silent-egress mechanism: because every cross-boundary movement of every other class must write a witnessed ledger entry here first, no personal data can cross a boundary without leaving an immutable, user-visible trace.
work_items.sync_edge: every consent/access/egress/deletion on DC-01..11 -> append-only PostgreSQL ledger (forward-only, witnessed)

family_invariants: User sovereignty: for every class the user is the ownership, consent, sharing, and deletion authority; the STORE-001 store_owner is the technical durable custodian, not the rights-holder.

Single durable owner: each class names exactly one canonical durable owner (PostgreSQL, OS keyring, AgentDB, or - for the raw presence stream - redb as its intended terminal transient role); no class has two durable writers.
Secrets never co-mingle: raw credentials/keys/tokens live ONLY in the OS keyring; PostgreSQL and redb are barred from holding secret material (PERSONAL-DC-02, and the token portions of DC-04/DC-10).
Forward-only sync: transient (redb) -> envctl(embed) -> durable (PostgreSQL); no undirected dual-write; derived filesystem projections are read-only from PostgreSQL.

No silent egress: every consent, access, cross-boundary movement, and deletion is a mandatory witnessed entry in the PERSONAL-DC-12 ledger; nothing crosses a boundary without a user-visible, tamper-evident trace.
No sidecar: personal semantics/graph (health trends, contact relationships, message search) live in RuVector inside PostgreSQL - there is no external vector store to exfiltrate from.
Provenance preserved: SHA-256 (artifacts/blobs) and SHAKE256 (witness chains) identity plus source lineage on every durable class; Causal Graph Verification rejects unrooted (hallucinated) edits.
Verified cutover: any personal class currently on redb-durable moves to PostgreSQL only after a green blobstore_parity.rs (SHA-256 identity) run for that class; never by assumption (STORE-001).
Transient means transient: raw presence/location and raw biometric samples are not backed up or recoverable by design - only settled, significant, consented states persist.

proof_tasks.asserts: Each of the 12 classes names exactly one canonical durable owner and one user authority; no class has two durable writers.
Every class has an explicit consent gate; multi-party classes (DC-09) require multi-party consent; ingestion/sharing without a consent grant is barred.
proof_tasks.method: assert work_items[].consent non-empty and DC-09 multi-party

proof_tasks.asserts: Every class maps to a STORE-001 state class with a named canonical owner + transient role + lifetime.
proof_tasks.method: assert each class id appears in store_001_alignment.state_class_mapping

proof_tasks.asserts: Every class has an at-rest boundary with key custody in the OS keyring; secrets never reach PostgreSQL/redb plaintext.
proof_tasks.method: assert work_items[].encryption non-empty and DC-02 keyring-only

proof_tasks.asserts: Every class has a retention tier; transient (redb) classes explicitly do not persist raw streams.
proof_tasks.method: assert work_items[].retention non-empty; DC-03/DC-09 raw-stream transient

proof_tasks.asserts: Every durable class has a recovery source (PostgreSQL PITR / keyring / regenerable); transient classes declare non-recoverable-by-design.
No class can cross a boundary without a witnessed PERSONAL-DC-12 ledger entry; no sidecar; secrets never egress.
proof_tasks.method: assert work_items[].egress_control non-empty and DC-12 is the enforcing substrate

proof_tasks.asserts: Raw secrets/tokens live only in the OS keyring; PostgreSQL/redb hold references only.
proof_tasks.method: assert DC-02 canonical_owner==keyring and DC-04/DC-10 token isolation

proof_tasks.asserts: Every durable class carries SHA-256/SHAKE256 identity + source lineage; unrooted edits are rejected.
Live chat (aiMessages) is excluded from persistence and cannot leak.
proof_tasks.method: assert DC-11 chat excluded from LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS

next_action: Decompose each PERSONAL-DC into domain workflows (health, finance, legal, files, calendar, contacts, family, comms) only after this class contract is proof-passed.
completion_gate: Every personal data class (PERSONAL-DC-01..12) has explicit ownership, consent, storage, encryption, access, retention, export, recovery, and a proof task; no data leaves its boundary silently (enforced by the PERSONAL-DC-12 witnessed egress ledger); PERSONAL-001.proof.json records product_execution_performed=false and STORE-001 alignment.
